states.question: What is the time complexity of two consecutive (non-nested) loops of size N?
states.explanation: Consecutive loops run sequentially: N + N = 2N, which asymptotically simplifies to O(N).

states.analogy_description: If you have two stations where station A processes parts, and then station B processes them, the time is additive. But if for every item at station A, B must inspect every other item, it becomes multiplicative (nested).

whiteboard_data.title: Nested Loop Index Iterations
whiteboard_data.description: Inner loop execution loops completely for each outer loop step.

states.exercise_description: Write a function `sum_n(n)` that returns the sum of all integers from 1 to `n` using a single loop (Demonstrates O(N) time).
Write a function `count_pairs(n)` that calculates how many pairs (i, j) exist such that 0 <= i < j < n. Use nested loops (Demonstrates O(N^2) time).

states.prompt: Explain Program Analysis in your own words to a peer. Use an analogy from daily life.

flashcards.front: What does auxiliary space complexity mean?
flashcards.back: The extra memory allocated by the algorithm, excluding the inputs.

flashcards.front: What is the worst-case space complexity of recursion?
flashcards.back: O(D), where D is the maximum recursion depth (due to call stack frames).
